folio.label: 149r
folio.l: Thow art so fals , and so vnkynde allas
Now goodemen , god for ©eue ©ow ©oure trespas
And ware ©ow fro the synne , of Auarice
Myn holy ¹dou~ , may ©ow alle waryce
So that ©e offre nobles , or starlynges
Or elles , siluer , broches , spones , Rynges
Bowetú ©oure hed , vnder this holy bulle
Come vp ©e wyues , and offritú of ©oure wolle
ñoure names I entre , heere in my rolle a noon
In to the blisse of heuene , shuln ©e gon
I ©ow assoile , be myn heigú powere
ñe that wyln offre , as clene and eke as clere
As ©e were born~ , and loo sires thus I preche
And Iesu Crist , that is oure soules leche
So graunte ©ow , his pardou~ to resseyue
For that is best , I wol ©ow nat disseyue

note.notetxt: 590 There is a one-line gap after this line.Est
590 Gap perhaps due to the progress of copying. Pricking on the left side next to the bODR

folio.l: But Sires a word , forgat I in my tale
I haue Relikes , and pardou~ in my male
As faire , as any man , in Engelond
Whicú were me ©euen , by the Popes hond
If any of ©ow , wol of deuocioun
Offryn , and han myn absolucioun
Come fortú a noon , and knelitú heere a doun
And mekely receyuetú , myn ¹doun
Or elles takitú pardoun , as ©e wende
Al newe and fressú , at euäy myles ende
So that ©e offren alwey , newe and newe
Nobles or pans , whicú that ben goode & trewe
It is an honox , to euäycú that is heere
That ©e moun haue , a suffisaunt Pardonere
To assoile ©ow , in contre as ©e ride
For auentures , whicú that moun betide
îauenture there moun falle , on or two
Doun of his hors , & breke his nekke a two
Loke whicú a seurte , is it to ©ow alle
That I am in ©oure felaweshipe , I falle
That may assoile ©ow , bothe more and lasse
Whan that the soule , shal fro the body passe
I rede that oure hoost , shal begynne
For he is moost envoluped , in Synne
Come fortú Sire hoost , and offre first a noon
